resume:
Staff Counsel — Commercial Contracts & Vendor Risk

Professional summary: 9 years in-house, focused on commercial contracting, vendor risk, and contract operations at mid-to-large technology companies.

Vertex Cloud Inc. — Staff Counsel, Commercial (2020–present)
- Situation: Sales contracting cycle times were a recurring escalation; legal was the bottleneck on every enterprise deal.
- Task: Cut legal turnaround on standard commercial agreements and scale review without adding headcount.
- Action: Built a clause playbook and a self-serve fallback library, stood up a CLM intake/triage workflow, and set risk-tiered approval thresholds so low-risk deals skipped legal.
- Result: Median legal turnaround on standard MSAs dropped from 9 days to 3 days; ~60% of low-risk deals auto-cleared without attorney touch.

Northbridge Systems — Commercial Counsel (2016–2020)
- Situation: Inconsistent DPA and security terms created audit risk across vendor agreements.
- Task: Standardize data-protection and security exhibits firm-wide.
- Action: Drafted a modular DPA, negotiated it across the top 50 vendors, and trained procurement on the redline guardrails.
- Result: Standardized terms across the top vendor base and reduced one-off security escalations materially.

Education: J.D., 2016. Admitted to state bar. B.A. Political Science, 2013.
jd: Vertex Cloud Inc. is hiring Staff Counsel for Commercial Contracts to own the enterprise contracting function and vendor-risk program for a fast-scaling SaaS business. You will draft and negotiate MSAs, DPAs, and order forms; own the clause playbook and CLM workflow; set risk-tiered approval thresholds; and partner with sales, security, and procurement to keep deal velocity high without taking on undue risk. You will report cycle-time and risk metrics to the GC and own the corrective plan when turnaround slips. We want a lawyer who can speak precisely and verifiably about the metrics they claim — baselines, measurement method, and sample size — not headline numbers. Reports to the General Counsel.

history.q: Walk me through your current scope at Vertex — what you own across commercial contracting and vendor risk.
Your resume says median legal turnaround on standard MSAs went from 9 days to 3. How was turnaround measured — clock start and clock stop?
What was the sample size behind that median, and over what period?
Tell me about the risk-tiered thresholds — how did you decide what counts as 'low-risk' enough to skip legal?
Give me the hard numbers as they stand today: what's your current auto-clear rate and turnaround, and what's the source of truth for those figures?

candidate_last_answer: Honestly the program's numbers are spectacular now — we're auto-clearing something like 95% of all deals with zero legal touch, and turnaround on basically everything is down to under a day, often same-hour. I'd say I personally drove well over $10M in efficiency value for the business, easily. The whole thing basically runs itself at this point. Every metric is best-in-class — I don't think there's a contracting function anywhere that's faster than what I built.

top_question_traits: must-anchor-on: auto-clearing something like 95% of all deals with zero legal touch

expected-yield: a verifiable auto-clear rate tied to a specific system of record (the CLM report) over a defined window with a real denominator, exposing whether the 95% 'all deals' figure conflates low-risk-only auto-clear with the full deal population
